label: 3+ children / Medium / Chaotic
description: Three kids all doing different things at once
transcript: So it's Saturday morning and all three of them are just um doing their own thing and it's beautiful chaos like Lila is at the table painting something and there's paint everywhere and Noah is on the floor building some kind of tower with every single block we own and baby Jude is just sitting in his bouncer watching them both going back and forth like he's watching a tennis match and every once in a while he just squeals with delight and Lila goes Jude likes my painting and Noah goes no he likes my tower and Jude just keeps squealing at both of them

label: Newborn / Short / Sentimental
description: Parent with brand new baby, first days home
transcript: Jude is four days old and I'm just sitting here holding him at two in the morning and the house is completely quiet and he's making these tiny little sounds in his sleep like little sighs and his whole hand is wrapped around my one finger and I just keep thinking you were inside me a week ago and now you're here and you're real and you're so small

label: Toddler destruction / Short / Funny
description: Kid makes an enormous mess with creative logic
transcript: Okay so I left the room for literally two minutes and Noah got into the flour in the pantry and when I came back he was covered head to toe and the kitchen looked like it snowed and I said Noah what happened and he goes I was making a beach for my dinosaurs and sure enough there's like five little dinosaurs lined up on a pile of flour on the floor

label: Older child / Medium / Bittersweet
description: Kid is growing up and doesn't want to hold hands anymore
transcript: We were walking into the store today and I reached for Lila's hand like I always do and she kind of pulled away and said mom I'm seven I don't need to hold hands anymore and I know she's right and I know it's normal but we've been holding hands walking into that store since she was two and I don't know when the last time was that she reached for my hand first but I wish I had noticed it because that was the last time and I didn't even know

label: Twins / Short / Funny
description: Twin toddlers being hilariously in sync
transcript: The twins did it again where they just looked at each other across the room and then simultaneously started running toward the dog like they planned it telepathically and the dog saw them coming and just bolted and they both laughed the exact same laugh at the exact same time and I'm telling you these two share one brain

label: Nonverbal moment / Short / Sentimental
description: No words spoken, just a gesture
transcript: Nothing was said but Noah came downstairs this morning while I was having a bad day and he just sat next to me on the couch and put his head on my shoulder and stayed there for like five minutes didn't say anything didn't ask for anything just sat with me

label: Failed attempt / Medium / Funny
description: Kid tries to do something nice but it goes hilariously wrong
transcript: So Lila decided she was going to make me breakfast in bed for Mother's Day and she got up early and um she tried to make toast but she couldn't figure out the toaster so she put bread in the microwave and it got all rubbery and then she poured orange juice but missed the glass entirely and it went all over the counter and she brought me this tray with rubbery bread a wet napkin and an empty glass and she was so proud and I ate every single bite and told her it was the best breakfast I'd ever had

label: Embarrassing public moment / Short / Funny
description: Kid says something mortifying in public
transcript: We're in the checkout line at the grocery store and it's completely silent and Noah points at the man in front of us and goes mama that man has a really big nose and I wanted the floor to swallow me whole and the man just turned around and laughed and said you know what buddy you're right and Noah goes see mama I told you

label: 3 children / Medium / Sweet teamwork
description: All three kids work together on something
transcript: Okay this never happens but all three kids just spent the last hour building a blanket fort together and Lila was the architect telling everyone where to put the pillows and Noah was doing the heavy lifting dragging chairs from the kitchen and baby Jude's only contribution was sitting in the middle of the floor chewing on a blanket corner but they made him the king of the fort and put a little crown on him that Lila made from construction paper and they're all in there right now with flashlights reading stories well Lila's reading and Noah's looking at pictures and Jude is eating the crown

label: Spiritual / Deep / Short
description: Kid says something unexpectedly profound about death or heaven
transcript: Noah asked about great grandma today and why she's not here anymore and I told him she passed away and she's in heaven now and he got really quiet and then said do you think she can see us from there and I said I think so and he said then I'm going to wave at her every night before bed just in case and I'm just completely wrecked right now

label: Sensory / texture focused / Short
description: Kid discovers something tactile for the first time
transcript: Jude touched grass for the first time today like actually sat on a blanket outside and then crawled off it and put his hands in the grass and the look on his face was pure shock like what is this stuff and he kept picking it up and letting it fall through his fingers and then he tried to eat it obviously

label: Discipline moment / Medium / Tender
description: After a tough discipline moment, connection happens
transcript: Noah was in timeout for hitting his sister and he was so angry and when the timer went off I went and sat down next to him and I said are you ready to talk about it and he just burst into tears and said I'm sorry mama I don't know why I did it I don't want to be mean and I held him and told him that feeling bad about it means he has a good heart and that everyone makes mistakes and he said even you and I said especially me and he wiped his face and said okay I'm going to tell Lila sorry and then he went and found her and hugged her without me asking

label: Tiny voice / Short / Sentimental
description: Recording specifically to capture how they sound at this age
transcript: I'm just recording this because I want to remember what Lila's voice sounds like right now at age three she's sitting here telling me about her day and she says her r's like w's and she calls spaghetti pasketti and butterflies are flutterbies and I just know one day she's going to talk like a regular person and I'm going to miss this so much

label: Multiple kids / Long / Bedtime chaos
description: Bedtime routine with three kids is absolute mayhem
transcript: Bedtime with three kids is an Olympic sport I swear okay so first Jude needs to nurse and while I'm doing that Lila comes in and says she can't find her stuffed bunny and it's an emergency and Noah takes this opportunity to sneak out of bed and go back downstairs because he forgot to tell me something important and the important thing is that he saw a lizard today and I'm like great buddy get back in bed and then Lila finds the bunny it was under her pillow the whole time and Jude finally falls asleep and I go to put him down and Noah has gotten out of bed AGAIN because now his water is too warm and he needs cold water and I get the cold water and Lila calls out and says she needs me to check for monsters and I check for monsters and by the time everyone is finally down it's been an hour and forty five minutes and I collapse on the couch and my husband goes so how was bedtime and I just stare at him

label: Proud parent / Short / Milestone
description: Overhearing kid be kind to another kid without knowing parent is watching
transcript: I was picking Lila up from school and I got there a few minutes early and I could see her on the playground through the fence and there was a little boy sitting by himself and Lila walked over and sat down next to him and I couldn't hear what she said but after a minute he smiled and they both got up and went to play together and she had no idea I saw it and honestly I have never been more proud of her
